source: user request 2026-05-04 â€” 'over-engineer memory'; session log shows conflicting location facts ('lives in Lawrenceville' AND 'lives in Multan' both active)
title: L3 write path has no dedup or conflict detection â€” contradictory facts accumulate
what_failed: L3 contains both 'User lives in Lawrenceville, Georgia, USA' and 'User lives in Multan, Pakistan' as active permanent_profile entries. Both were written at different times; the write path had no check for contradictions. Additionally, stale project entries remain active ('Pi needs to implement memory deduplication') long after the work is done. The L3 write path only checks for exact-ID duplicates, not semantic/content duplicates.
where_failed: tools/tools_memory.py::memory_write() â€” no pre-write similarity or conflict check against existing L3 entries in the same category.
why_likely: Memory write was implemented as append-only. Dedup at write time was listed as a future enhancement (T-026 only did distillation dedup, not write-time dedup).
suggested_fix: Before inserting a new L3 entry: (1) load existing entries in same category from SQLite, (2) compute word-overlap ratio between new content and each existing entry, (3) if overlap > 0.7 with a shorter entry, UPDATE that entry instead of inserting, (4) if new entry CONTRADICTS an existing entry (detectable by opposite-polarity keywords: 'lives in X' vs earlier 'lives in Y'), soft-delete the old entry and log the conflict. Return conflict_detected flag in memory_write result.
